Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff'S Deposition Proceedings Re [1] Cease "Speaking Objections", Coaching Of Witness And Unprofessional Interruptions And [2] Compel Further Deposition Of Plaintiff ; Declararions Of Darren P. Roach And Ann K. Loper In Support And Separate Statement In Support
Off Calendar Apr-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Darren P. Roach, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Pacific Gas and Electric Company
Alec Segarich, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Maria Sutton

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Pacific Gas & Electric Company's motion for protective order re: plaintiff's deposition proceedings; and to compel further deposition of plaintiff denied in part, granted in part:
a. motion for specific protective orders re: conduct of counsel denied;
b. motion to compel further deposition of plaintiff granted; further deposition of plaintiff shall be conducted in afternoon sessions, between 1:30 p.m. and 4:30 p.m. in Department 610; the parties shall notify the court in advance of the dates of continued deposition, to ensure that the courtroom is available. Commissioner: Paul Slavit, Court Reporter: Niki Makela, CSR # 11024

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Order Of Contempt Against Pltf'S Counsel'S Improper Language And Conduct
Off Calendar Apr-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Darren P. Roach, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Pacific Gas and Electric Company
Alec Segarich, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Maria Sutton

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Motion for protective order and contempt: off calendar per court's order, pending completion of pending deposition(s); if problems in the conduct of depositions re-occur, defendant may restore the motion to calendar, and submit supplemental declarations, if appropriate, to support a request for contempt or sanctions
Commissioner: Paul Slavit; Court Reporter: Niki Makela, CSR # 11024

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff Maria Sutton Declarations Of Paul S.D Berg, Ph.D; Margo M. Leahy, M.D; And Darren P. Roach In Support
Off Calendar Apr-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Darren P. Roach, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Pacific Gas and Electric Company
Alec Segarich, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Maria Sutton

After hearing, Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's Motion to compel independent medical examination of plaintiff is Granted. Testing and examination shall be conducted on May 8, 2009 or as agreed by counsel. The IME may be audio recorded but counsel shall not be present. Commissioner: Paul Slavit; Court Reporter: Niki Makela, CSR # 11024

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Order Of Contempt Against Pltf'S Counsel'S Improper Language And Conduct

No appearance, Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric company's Motion for protective order and contempt continued to April 28, 2009, to be heard with defendant's pending motions for protective order, and to compel defense medical examination. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Alec Segarich, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Maria Sutton

After hearing, Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's Motion to compel production of documents from plaintiff and to compel continued deposition of plaintiff is Denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notie Of Motion For Protectiveorder Precluding The Depositions Relating To Senior Officers
Off Calendar Apr-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Darren Roach, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Pacific Gas & Electric Company

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Pacific Gas & Electric Company's motion for protective order precluding the depositions of senior officers granted, as follows:
a. Until further order of this court, plaintiff shall be precluded from taking the deposition of defendant's senior executives Helen Burt, John Simon and Peter Darbee;
b. At any time, plaintiff may apply for relief from this protective order by noticed motion, and with a showing of good cause why the deposition of a senior officer should be permitted;
c. Nothing in this order shall prevent plaintiff from propounding other discovery in furtherance of her claim, or from taking the deposition of a person most knowledgeable, person most qualified, or custodian of records to obtain information or documents concerning defendant's policies and procedures, or other pertinent information concerning plaintiff's claim. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
